svg.text: How chizu maps a codebase into a navigable graph for AI agents

g.text: Every Rust workspace maps to a hierarchical graph of entities connected by typed edges
Each entity stores: name, kind, path, lines, visibility
Each entity can carry additional data that agents read during navigation
what code does without reading it
An agent receives a task, then navigates the graph to find, understand, and modify the right code
"Add rate limiting to the API endpoints"
find the relevant part of the codebase
routes.rs (3 symbols) middleware.rs (2 symbols) → core dependency
Rate limiting should go in middleware.rs, applied before handle_request
chizu index . → graph updated with new RateLimiter symbol
sha256 detects changed files, only re-parses what changed
Why a graph, not just file search?
Agents don't grep blindly. They start
at a component, follow edges to find
source units and symbols. The graph
Instead of scanning 100 files, an agent
visits 3 nodes to reach the right code.
other code might be affected by a
the agent checks what depends on it.
guessed. Agents know the blast radius.
The graph persists in SQLite. Agents

svg.text: chizu · Rust workspace → knowledge graph → agent-navigable codebase map
